expectations.text: Explains the rationale for accommodating resistance (matching the force/strength curve)
expectations.evidence: Explains: 'resistance increases as you move into your stronger range.'

expectations.text: Provides a concrete example (lighter at bottom, heavier near lockout)
expectations.evidence: Example: '250 lbs of bar weight plus 50 lbs of band tension at the top.'

expectations.text: Cites research or meta-analytic evidence showing little to no clear advantage
expectations.evidence: States: 'no significant difference in strength development when total training volume is equated.'

expectations.text: Concludes bands/chains are fine as variation tools but not reliably superior
expectations.evidence: States: 'roughly equivalent to straight weight for building absolute strength when volume is controlled.'
